texto: This is therefore a time, not for unkindly criticism of fellow Christians, but for friendly conference; not for disputing over divergent views, but for united action; not for dogmatic assertion of prophetic programs, but for the humble acknowledgment that “we know in part;” not for idle dreaming, but for the immediate task of evangelizing a lost world. For such effort, no one truth is more inspiring, than that of the return of Christ. None other can make us sit more lightly by the things of time, none other is more familiar as a Scriptural motive to purity, holiness, patience, vigilance, love.
explicacao: Citação de Charles R. Erdman sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “This is therefore a time, not for unkindly criticism of fellow Christians, but for friendly confe…” articula motivacao, christianity, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e termino.
